seo.title: AI Code Generation implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping AI Code Generation projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides a rigorous framework for validating AI-generated code before it reaches production. It focuses on mitigating risks associated with hallucinations, security vulnerabilities, and technical debt specific to large language model outputs.

items.title: Secrets and Credential Scanning
items.description: Run a tool like gitleaks or trufflehog on all AI-generated patches to ensure no placeholder API keys or hardcoded credentials were included.

items.title: Dependency Version Verification
items.description: Cross-reference AI-suggested library versions against the project's lockfile or a vulnerability database (Snyk/OSV) to prevent dependency confusion attacks or use of deprecated packages.

items.title: Injection Vulnerability Audit
items.description: Verify that all generated SQL queries, shell commands, or HTML outputs use parameterized inputs rather than string concatenation to prevent SQLi and XSS.

items.title: SCA and SAST Execution
items.description: Run Static Analysis Security Testing (SAST) specifically on the files modified by AI to detect common patterns like unsafe deserialization or weak cryptographic algorithms.

items.title: Permission and Scope Review
items.description: Verify that AI-generated logic for authorization or file system access adheres to the principle of least privilege.

items.title: Automated Unit Test Coverage
items.description: Ensure every generated function has at least one corresponding unit test covering the primary success path and failure modes.

items.title: Edge Case Boundary Testing
items.description: Manually add tests for null inputs, empty strings, and maximum integer values to verify AI-generated logic handles boundary conditions correctly.

items.title: Hallucination Verification
items.description: Verify that any called internal APIs or third-party methods actually exist in the current versions of the libraries used.

items.title: Loop and Recursion Analysis
items.description: Inspect AI-generated loops and recursive calls to ensure exit conditions are unreachable-proof and do not cause stack overflows or infinite loops.

items.title: Integration Test Pass
items.description: Execute full integration test suites to confirm that AI-generated modules interact correctly with existing database schemas and network services.

items.title: Linter and Formatter Compliance
items.description: Run ESLint, Prettier, or language-equivalent tools to ensure AI-generated code matches the team's formatting and style guidelines.

items.title: Type Safety Check
items.description: Run TypeScript compiler or MyPy to ensure AI-generated types are consistent and do not rely on 'any' or 'ignore' pragmas.

items.title: Dead Code Removal
items.description: Identify and remove unused variables, unreachable branches, or redundant imports often left behind by iterative AI prompting.

items.title: Docstring and Comment Accuracy
items.description: Verify that generated comments correctly describe the logic and are not outdated residues from previous prompt iterations.

items.title: Naming Convention Audit
items.description: Ensure variable and function names follow the project's specific naming patterns (e.g., camelCase vs snake_case).

items.title: Model Context Configuration
items.description: Verify that .cursorrules or .github/copilot-instructions files are updated with the latest architectural patterns to guide the AI effectively.

items.title: Prompt Versioning
items.description: For complex tasks, document the system prompt or multi-step sequence used to generate the code for future reproducibility.

items.title: External Documentation Indexing
items.description: Ensure the AI tool has access to the latest documentation versions for niche libraries via @docs or similar context-loading features.

items.title: Model Selection Verification
items.description: Confirm that the most capable model (e.g., Claude 3.5 Sonnet or GPT-4o) was used for complex logic rather than faster, smaller models.

items.title: Environment Parity Check
items.description: Verify the AI was prompted with the correct runtime environment (e.g., Node 20 vs Node 16) to avoid syntax errors.

items.title: AI Attribution Tagging
items.description: Label Pull Requests or commits with an 'AI-generated' tag to alert reviewers to look for specific LLM failure modes.

items.title: Mandatory Human Sign-off
items.description: Require at least one human peer review for any AI-generated code that touches the data persistence or authentication layers.

items.title: Refactoring Verification
items.description: When using AI for refactoring, perform a side-by-side diff to ensure no functional logic was accidentally altered during the cleanup.

items.title: Performance Benchmarking
items.description: Run a performance profile on AI-generated algorithms to ensure they do not introduce O(n^2) or worse complexity where O(n) is expected.

items.title: Legal and License Compliance
items.description: Check generated code against open-source license filters (e.g., Copilot's public code filter) to avoid GPL contamination in proprietary repos.
